title: Configurable Access Token Lifetime per Credential
description:
AI agents running long-horizon tasks (e.g. a multi-step data pipeline, a scheduled job, or an orchestrator coordinating sub-agents) are forced to interrupt their workflow every 900 seconds to refresh tokens. Unlike human users who can respond to a login prompt, an agent that fails to refresh mid-task silently receives 401s and must implement its own retry/refresh loop — adding error-prone boilerplate to every agent that uses the service.

This task introduces a per-credential token lifetime configuration so that agents (or their operators) can tune the access token expiry to match the expected task duration.

Required changes:
1. Add an optional `token_lifetime_seconds` field to the `CredentialCreate` schema (integer, minimum 60, maximum 86400 i.e. 24h, default null = use server default of 900).
2. When a token is minted via `client_credentials` grant using a credential that has `token_lifetime_seconds` set, use that value for the access token `exp` instead of the server default.
3. Surface the effective `token_lifetime_seconds` in the `CredentialResponse` so agents can introspect their own credential configuration.
4. The `refresh_before` field in `TokenResponse` must be computed correctly relative to the actual token lifetime (60 seconds before expiry).
5. Server-side maximum must be enforced — requests for `token_lifetime_seconds > 86400` must be rejected with 422 and a clear error message.
validation: Token minted with a custom-lifetime credential has exp matching the configured lifetime
CredentialResponse includes token_lifetime_seconds field (null if using server default)
refresh_before in TokenResponse is always 60s before the actual token expiry
token_lifetime_seconds > 86400 is rejected with 422 and descriptive error
token_lifetime_seconds < 60 is rejected with 422 and descriptive error
Omitting token_lifetime_seconds falls back to server default (900s)
Existing credentials without token_lifetime_seconds continue to issue 900s tokens
Integration test: credential with token_lifetime_seconds=3600 issues token with exp ~3600s in the future
Integration test: credential with token_lifetime_seconds=100000 is rejected at creation time
